text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver is China's stronger-than-expected factory output on tech export demand — that's a direct tailwind for semiconductors and your chip-heavy book. Iran deal is cooling oil and geopolitical fear, which frees up risk appetite for growth names.

Your holdings: SOXL is the one staring you down at -7.06% — it's a 3x levered semiconductor ETF that decays daily, and on margin that hole gets deeper fast; your stop is $200, price is $236, so you still have buffer but watch it closely. SMH is also slightly underwater at -0.69%, less urgent but same sector risk. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — tiny position or tight hands required; margin amplifies any sudden drop hard.

Watch: 1) Semiconductor price action today — China tech demand news could spark a bounce in SMH/SOXL, good moment to reassess SOXL size. 2) Yen weakness — a 40-year low raises intervention risk, which could spike volatility and hit risk assets fast.

Bottom line: SOXL is your live grenade — decide today if the position size makes sense on margin.
